Lake superior agate that has been polished with a tumbler and oil. It was found by Seth Trobec near Duluth Minnesota. An agate is an ornamental stone consisting of a hard variety of chalcedony, typically banded in appearance. Beautiful!

a friend gave me some vintage rock & mineral magazines and this brochure was tucked inside. no date, but the design/printing looks early-mid ‘70s and references the lake superior agate becoming our state gemstone in 1969

Here's one of my favorite Lake Superior agate finds of all time. I found this north of Paradise, Michigan rockhounding with a dear friend Rose LaPere and my wife ca. 1984. (approx. size 1.5" x 1.25"). #lakesuperioragate, #michigan, #puremichigan, #rocks&minerals, #agate, #photography, #gemstone

One cool thing about having bought a property that was covered in River Rock and a small child and dogs that have scattered it all over and buried it is that every once in a while I'll be walking around and find a chunk of Lake Superior agate in my own back yard!

Also, despite the fact that I found this on the shores of Lake Superior, I don't believe it would be considered a Lake Superior agate. It has no red in it from the iron around here, so I do wonder about its (millions of years) history.

HMMM ... some look like Lake Superior agate slices ... now you got me wonderin' about my favorite sport ... sittin' on the beach perusing all the rocks ... could they be related ... well, ya know, distant cousins ... of course, very distant 😁
Cleaning a very dirty agate for the first time-Lake Superior Agate

So I've been a rockhound since I was a kid, and I love agates (duh), so it's pretty sweet to see one of my agate photos on the cover of the Moose Lake Visitor's Guide! It's the #agate capital of the world, and it's also the home of Agate Days, which is a lot of fun. #geology #agates #mooselake

It's so cool. In fact, Agate Harbor in the town of Copper Harbor is probably unique in the world in that instead of a sandy bottom it has a rust red rocky shelf shot through with pink & green veins of quartz. The Lake Superior shoreline is a geological paradise in addition to a boreal one.

It looks like brown jasper since it looks opaque with subtle striations. It’s probably not agate since agate is usually more translucent to transparent with more defined banding. Do you happen to live anywhere near Lake Superior? There’s lots of both brown agate and brown jasper there.

Y'all, I've got agate fever. There's nothing like combing the beach and finding the vibrant lines and colors of a lake superior agate. Ok, maybe hunting, fishing, and finding good Mexican food can all compete. But agate hunting and gardening and my things now.
Yes Sabine! LSA Lake Superior Agate, regionally known for the reddish hue, which comes from forming in an iron rich area…so I am told.
You ain’t gonna find no Lake Superior agate over there Brad. Only within a few hundred miles of where I do be livin
